  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": [],

  "confidence": 0.95,

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Guards and access control shown in pre-task photos but no verification they meet OSHA 1910.212(a)(1) requirements for nip points, shear points, and rotating parts.",
    "Daily briefing form signed without confirmation of active exposure assessment, creating potential for complacency on a moving conveyor.",
    "No evidence of lockout/tagout or zero-energy verification during in-progress work on conveyor systems."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately stop work and verify all guards are in place and meet the specific requirements of OSHA 1910.212(a)(1) for the conveyor's pinch points and rotating components.",
    "Conduct a live exposure assessment and update the daily briefing to reflect current conditions, including identification of all energy sources and required LOTO procedures.",
    "Confirm that access control (e.g., barriers, signage) is fully intact and effective before resuming any work."
  ],
  "rationale": "In-progress work on a conveyor system with only a signed briefing and pre-task photos does not guarantee that guards remain compliant or that energy isolation is active. OSHA 1910.212 requires effective guarding at the point of operation, and CEMA standards emphasize continuous protection during maintenance and operation. The absence of verified energy isolation during active work represents a critical hazard.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.212(a)(1) - Machine Guarding General Requirements",
    "CEMA Standard 402 (Belt Conveyors) - Safety Requirements"
  ]
}
